title: China drives increased bauxite flows, but Guinea's cutback could drag on growth
section_path: China drives increased bauxite flows, but Guinea's cutback could drag on growth
section_path_text: China drives increased bauxite flows, but Guinea's cutback could drag on growth
summary:
Main
India has emerged as a key destination for bauxite as risks in the Arabian Gulf rise. Bauxite carrying Capesize tonne-miles from Signal Ocean
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-05-19_china-drives-increased-bauxite-flows-but-guineas-cutback-could-drag-on-growth_img_unnamed_2d177c5ad9b0.png
Key Takeaways
● Global bauxite flows increased by 24% y/y in April. ● Flows to China increased by 26% y/y.

children.title: Guinea's approach could shift how capes perform in 2026 H2
section_path: Guinea's approach could shift how capes perform in 2026 H2
children.section_path_text: Guinea's approach could shift how capes perform in 2026 H2
children.summary: Looking ahead, the bauxite market outlook is broadly constructive but increasingly bifurcated by risk. China's sustained import growth provides a reliable demand floor, while India's rise as a destination market is structural rather than purely opportunistic, underpinned by a multi-year alumina refinery expansion pipeline from Vedanta and Hindalco that will continue to draw seaborne bauxite regardless of Arabian Gulf normalisation.
